mnemonics: DONALD: Don't Overlook Necessary Assumptions for Linear Duality
Donald Duck Hunts For Optimal Ducks (Duality, Hessian, Feasibility, Optimality, Duality Gap, Karush-Kuhn-Tucker)
keyPoints: Duality Theory: Every linear programming problem (the primal) has a corresponding dual problem, and the solutions are deeply connected.
Optimality Conditions: The Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions provide necessary and sufficient criteria for a solution to be optimal in convex programming.
Sensitivity Analysis: Understanding how changes in the problem's parameters (like constraints or coefficients) affect the optimal solution.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check the constraint qualification when applying KKT conditions, which are only necessary for optimality if the problem satisfies certain regularity conditions.
Confusing the signs of the dual variables (shadow prices) or misinterpreting the economic meaning of the dual solution.
Assuming strong duality holds in all optimization problems; it is guaranteed in linear programming but can fail in non-convex problems.
